title: vitis vulpina var. parvifolia
intro: Vitis vulpina var. parvifolia, also known as the sand grape, is a type of grapevine native to eastern North America, characterized by its small, edible fruit and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The health benefits of Vitis vulpina var. parvifolia include its ability to support the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its fruit can be eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
